title: Materialized Lake Views (MLV) in Fabric Lakehouses
summary: Materialized Lake Views are pre-computed Delta table snapshots inside a Fabric Lakehouse that accelerate repeated query patterns. They are refreshed on demand or on a schedule, and their refresh behavior is controlled by MLV execution definitions.

concept.what_is_mlv: A Materialized Lake View (MLV) is a Delta table computed from a query over other lakehouse tables. Fabric can refresh it automatically so downstream consumers always read current data without re-running the underlying query.
concept.execution_definition_role: An MLV execution definition groups the refresh scope, Spark environment, and refresh strategy for one or more materialized views. A refresh schedule then references the execution definition by ID via executionData.mlvExecutionDefinitionId, decoupling the schedule (when to refresh) from the execution parameters (how to refresh).

refresh_modes.Optimal: Default. Fabric decides whether to run an incremental or full refresh based on Delta log state.
refresh_modes.Full: Force a full recomputation on every refresh, discarding incremental state.

execution_context_modes.All: Refresh every MLV in the lakehouse (or every lakehouse in the extended lineage group).
execution_context_modes.Selected: Refresh only the MLVs listed in selectedMlvs (fully-qualified names, e.g. 'schema.view_name') or lakehouses listed in selectedLakehouses.

create_schedule.note: Pass via --file or --content JSON. Use executionData.mlvExecutionDefinitionId to link to an execution definition.

recommended_workflow: 1. Create an MLV execution definition with the desired scope and refresh mode.
2. Capture the execution definition ID from the create response (data.id).
3. Create a refresh schedule referencing the definition ID via executionData.mlvExecutionDefinitionId.
4. Use refresh-materialized-views for ad-hoc refreshes outside the schedule.
what_agents_should_do: Use list-execution-definitions to discover existing definitions before creating new ones.
Pass JSON bodies via --content for simple inline payloads, or --file for complex objects.
Capture the execution definition ID from create output with --query data.id before creating a schedule.
Set refreshMode to Full only when incremental refresh would produce stale results (e.g., after major source schema changes).
Use Selected mode with selectedMlvs to limit refresh scope and reduce Spark compute cost.
To update only one field of an execution definition, pass a partial JSON body — only provided fields are updated (PATCH semantics).

Item references (settings.environment, extendedLineageExecutionContext.selectedLakehouses entries) use the discriminated ItemReferenceById shape: {"referenceType":"ById","itemId":"<uuid>","workspaceId":"<uuid>"} — not a bare {"id":...} object.

error_patterns.missing_required_fields: create-execution-definition returns 400 if displayName or currentLakehouseExecutionContext is missing.
error_patterns.invalid_fqn_in_selected_mode: selectedMlvs entries must be fully-qualified names matching the actual view names in the lakehouse. Typos return a 400 or 404.
error_patterns.definition_not_found: show/update/delete return 404 if the execution-definition-id does not exist. List first: fabio lakehouse list-execution-definitions --workspace $WS --id $LAKEHOUSE_ID
